tasks.title: Project Setup and Core Type Definitions
tasks.description: Initialize the TypeScript project with the specified dependency stack and create all Zod schemas for config, data storage, and scan state. This establishes the foundation that all other modules depend on.
tasks.details:
1. Initialize npm project and install all dependencies:
   - Runtime: @inquirer/prompts, chalk, commander, js-yaml, octokit, ora, simple-git, zod
   - Dev: @types/js-yaml, @types/node, tsup, tsx, typescript, vitest

2. Configure TypeScript (tsconfig.json):
   - Target ES2022, module NodeNext, strict mode enabled
   - Enable ESM with "type": "module" in package.json

3. Create `src/types/schema.ts` with Zod schemas:
   - MemberSchema: name, email (optional), aliases (array, default [])
   - TeamSchema: name, tag (default "default"), members array
   - OrgSchema: name, type ("core" | "consultant"), teams array
   - RepoSchema: path, name (optional), group (default "default")
   - ConfigSchema: repos, orgs, groups record, tags record, settings (weeks_back, staleness_minutes)
   - UserWeekRepoRecord interface: identity fields (member, email, org, orgType, team, tag), dimension fields (week, repo, group), metrics (commits, activeDays), filetype breakdown
   - CommitsByFiletype interface: version, lastUpdated, records array
   - ScanState interface: version, repos record with lastHash, lastScanDate, recentHashes, recordCount

4. Create `src/store/paths.ts`:
   - getConfigDir(): resolves ~/.agentx/gitradar/
   - getDataDir(): ~/.agentx/gitradar/data/
   - getConfigPath(), getCommitsPath(), getScanStatePath()
   - ensureDataDir(): creates directories if missing
   - Handle ~ expansion and path normalization
tasks.testStrategy:
Write unit tests for:
- All Zod schemas with valid and invalid input data
- Path resolution functions with mocked home directory
- Edge cases: missing optional fields, empty arrays, default values

subtasks.title: Initialize npm project and install all dependencies
subtasks.description: Set up the npm project foundation with package.json containing all runtime and development dependencies specified in the PRD.
subtasks.details:
1. Run `npm init` to create package.json with appropriate project metadata (name: agentx-gitradar, version: 0.1.0)
2. Install runtime dependencies: @inquirer/prompts, chalk, commander, js-yaml, octokit, ora, simple-git, zod
3. Install dev dependencies: @types/js-yaml, @types/node, tsup, tsx, typescript, vitest
4. Add bin entry pointing to dist/cli.js for global CLI usage
5. Add npm scripts: build (tsup), dev (tsx), test (vitest), typecheck (tsc --noEmit)
6. Verify all dependencies resolve correctly with `npm ls`
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify package.json structure is valid, all dependencies install without conflicts, and npm scripts execute without errors. Run `npm run typecheck` as a smoke test after TypeScript setup.

subtasks.title: Configure TypeScript and ESM module settings
subtasks.description: Create tsconfig.json with ES2022 target, NodeNext module resolution, and strict mode. Configure package.json for ESM compatibility.
subtasks.details:
1. Create tsconfig.json with:
   - compilerOptions: target ES2022, module NodeNext, moduleResolution NodeNext
   - strict: true, noEmit: true (we use tsup for builds)
   - outDir: ./dist, rootDir: ./src
   - include: ["src/**/*"], exclude: ["node_modules", "dist"]
2. Add "type": "module" to package.json for ESM
3. Create tsup.config.ts for bundling:
   - entry: ["src/cli.ts"], format: ["esm"], target: "node20"
   - dts: true for TypeScript declarations
   - shims: true for ESM compatibility
4. Create src/ directory structure with placeholder files
5. Verify ESM imports work with .js extensions in import statements
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `npm run typecheck` to verify TypeScript configuration. Create a minimal src/index.ts with an export and verify `npm run build` produces valid ESM output in dist/.

subtasks.title: Create Zod schemas for config, data types, and scan state
subtasks.description: Implement all Zod schemas in src/types/schema.ts for Member, Team, Org, Repo, Config, UserWeekRepoRecord, CommitsByFiletype, and ScanState types.
subtasks.details:
1. Create src/types/schema.ts with the following Zod schemas:
   - MemberSchema: z.object({ name: z.string(), email: z.string().email().optional(), aliases: z.array(z.string()).default([]) })
   - TeamSchema: z.object({ name: z.string(), tag: z.string().default("default"), members: z.array(MemberSchema) })
   - OrgSchema: z.object({ name: z.string(), type: z.enum(["core", "consultant"]), teams: z.array(TeamSchema) })
   - RepoSchema: z.object({ path: z.string(), name: z.string().optional(), group: z.string().default("default") })
   - ConfigSchema with repos array, orgs array, groups/tags records, settings (weeks_back, staleness_minutes)
2. Create TypeScript interfaces for data records:
   - UserWeekRepoRecord with identity fields (member, email, org, orgType, team, tag), dimensions (week, repo, group), metrics (commits, activeDays), filetype breakdown
   - CommitsByFiletype: { version: string, lastUpdated: string, records: UserWeekRepoRecord[] }
   - ScanState: { version: string, repos: Record<string, RepoScanState> } where RepoScanState has lastHash, lastScanDate, recentHashes[], recordCount
3. Export inferred TypeScript types using z.infer<typeof Schema>
subtasks.testStrategy: Write vitest unit tests in src/types/schema.test.ts: test valid data passes validation, invalid data throws ZodError with helpful messages, test default values are applied correctly, test optional fields can be omitted, test array fields handle empty arrays.

subtasks.title: Create path resolution utilities in src/store/paths.ts
subtasks.description: Implement path utilities for resolving config directory, data directory, and file paths with home directory (~) expansion and directory creation.
subtasks.details:
1. Create src/store/paths.ts with functions:
   - getConfigDir(): string - returns resolved path ~/.agentx/gitradar/
   - getDataDir(): string - returns ~/.agentx/gitradar/data/
   - getConfigPath(): string - returns ~/.agentx/gitradar/config.yml
   - getCommitsPath(): string - returns ~/.agentx/gitradar/data/commits-by-filetype.json
   - getScanStatePath(): string - returns ~/.agentx/gitradar/data/scan-state.json
2. Implement ensureDataDir(): Promise<void> - creates directories recursively using fs.mkdir with { recursive: true }
3. Create helper expandTilde(path: string): string using os.homedir() for ~ expansion
4. Handle path normalization using path.resolve() and path.normalize()
5. Export all functions for use by config loader and data storage modules
subtasks.testStrategy: Write vitest unit tests in src/store/paths.test.ts: mock os.homedir() to return a test path, verify all path functions return expected resolved paths, test tilde expansion works correctly, test ensureDataDir creates directories (use temp directory for testing), test path normalization handles edge cases like double slashes.

tasks.expansionPrompt: Break down into: (1) npm project initialization with all dependencies, (2) TypeScript/ESM configuration, (3) Zod schemas for config/data types in src/types/schema.ts, (4) Path resolution utilities in src/store/paths.ts with home directory expansion. Each subtask should include corresponding tests.

tasks.title: File Classifier and Config Loader
tasks.description: Implement the file path classifier that categorizes files into app/test/config/storybook types, and the YAML config loader with validation. These are core utilities used by the git collector.
tasks.details:
1. Create `src/collector/classifier.ts`:
   - classifyFile(filePath: string): "app" | "test" | "config" | "storybook"
   - Implement ordered rule matching (first match wins):
     Priority 1 (storybook): .stories.*, .story.*, .storybook/, .mdx in storybook paths
     Priority 2 (test): .test.*, .spec.*, .cy.*, .e2e.*, __tests__/, /tests?/, vitest.config, jest.config, cypress/, playwright/
     Priority 3 (config): .config.*, *.json, *.yml, *.yaml, *.toml, *.env*, Dockerfile, docker-compose, .github/, *.lock, Makefile, .eslintrc, .prettier*, tsconfig*, webpack.config, vite.config
     Priority 4 (app): everything else
   - Use regex patterns, test against basename and full path as needed

2. Define visual encoding constants:
   - FILETYPE_CHARS: { app: "█", test: "▓", config: "░", storybook: "▒" }
   - FILETYPE_COLORS: map to chalk.green, chalk.blue, chalk.yellow, chalk.magenta

3. Create `src/config/loader.ts`:
   - loadConfig(configPath?: string): Promise<Config>
   - Search default path ~/.agentx/gitradar/config.yml or use --config override
   - Parse YAML with js-yaml
   - Validate with ConfigSchema.parse()
   - Resolve ~ in repo paths, resolve relative paths against config location
   - Warn (console.warn) if repo path doesn't exist, don't crash
   - Return typed Config object
tasks.testStrategy:
Classifier tests:
- Test each file type category with multiple patterns
- Test priority ordering (.stories.test.tsx → storybook)
- Test edge cases: hidden files, nested paths, unusual extensions

Config loader tests:
- Valid YAML parsing and validation
- Invalid config rejection with helpful errors
- Path resolution with mocked filesystem
- Warning behavior for missing repos

subtasks.title: Implement file classifier with ordered regex rule matching
subtasks.description: Create src/collector/classifier.ts with classifyFile() function that categorizes file paths into app, test, config, or storybook types using priority-based regex pattern matching where first match wins.
subtasks.details:
1. Create src/collector/classifier.ts exporting:
   - FileType = 'app' | 'test' | 'config' | 'storybook'
   - classifyFile(filePath: string): FileType

2. Implement ordered rule matching (first match wins):
   Priority 1 (storybook): patterns for .stories.*, .story.*, .storybook/, .mdx in storybook paths
   Priority 2 (test): .test.*, .spec.*, .cy.*, .e2e.*, __tests__/, /tests?/, vitest.config, jest.config, cypress/, playwright/
   Priority 3 (config): .config.*, *.json, *.yml, *.yaml, *.toml, *.env*, Dockerfile, docker-compose, .github/, *.lock, Makefile, .eslintrc, .prettier*, tsconfig*, webpack.config, vite.config
   Priority 4 (app): default fallback for all other files

3. Use regex patterns tested against both basename (path.basename) and full path as needed. For example, __tests__/ requires full path check while .test.ts only needs basename.

4. Write comprehensive tests in src/collector/classifier.test.ts covering:
   - Each file type category with multiple pattern variations
   - Priority ordering edge cases (.stories.test.tsx → storybook, not test)
   - Hidden files (dotfiles), nested paths, unusual extensions
   - Path separators work cross-platform
subtasks.testStrategy: Write vitest tests covering: (1) app files: .ts, .tsx, .js, .py, .go, .css, .html, .md; (2) test files: .test.ts, .spec.tsx, component.cy.js, __tests__/utils.ts, tests/unit.js; (3) config files: .env.local, tsconfig.json, Dockerfile, .github/workflows/ci.yml, package-lock.json; (4) storybook files: Button.stories.tsx, Card.story.js, .storybook/main.js, docs.mdx in storybook paths; (5) priority edge cases: .stories.test.tsx → storybook, jest.config.ts → test (not config).

subtasks.title: Define visual encoding constants for file types
subtasks.description: Add visual encoding constants (FILETYPE_CHARS and FILETYPE_COLORS) for file type visualization using chalk colors and Unicode block characters.
subtasks.testStrategy: Write vitest tests verifying: (1) FILETYPE_CHARS has all 4 keys with correct Unicode characters; (2) FILETYPE_COLORS maps to chalk functions; (3) renderFileTypeChar returns ANSI-colored strings; (4) renderFileTypeLegend produces properly formatted inline legend string. Test that chalk functions are correctly applied by checking output contains ANSI escape codes.

subtasks.title: Implement YAML config loader with Zod validation
subtasks.description: Create src/config/loader.ts with loadConfig() function that reads YAML config file, parses with js-yaml, validates with ConfigSchema, and returns a typed Config object.
subtasks.details:
1. Create src/config/loader.ts exporting:
   - loadConfig(configPath?: string): Promise<Config>

2. Config file resolution logic:
   - Default path: ~/.agentx/gitradar/config.yml (use getConfigPath from store/paths.ts)
   - --config CLI option overrides default path
   - If config file doesn't exist, throw helpful error message

3. Config parsing flow:
   - Read file with fs.readFile (utf-8 encoding)
   - Parse YAML using js-yaml's load() function with safe schema
   - Validate parsed object with ConfigSchema.parse() from types/schema.ts
   - Handle Zod validation errors by transforming into user-friendly error messages

4. Error handling:
   - Missing config file: 'Config file not found at {path}. Run gitradar init or create config manually.'
   - Invalid YAML syntax: 'Invalid YAML in config file: {error}'
   - Schema validation failure: 'Config validation error: {zod error formatted}'

5. Write tests in src/config/loader.test.ts using mock filesystem (vi.mock('fs/promises')).
subtasks.testStrategy: Write vitest tests for: (1) valid YAML config parses and validates correctly; (2) missing file throws descriptive error; (3) malformed YAML throws syntax error; (4) schema validation failure shows Zod errors (missing required fields, invalid types, wrong enum values); (5) default path resolution works; (6) custom config path override works. Use vi.mock for fs/promises to control file reads.

subtasks.title: Implement path resolution for repo paths with tilde expansion
subtasks.description: Extend config loader to resolve repo paths including home directory (~) expansion and relative path resolution against the config file location, with warnings for non-existent paths.
subtasks.details:
1. Create path resolution helper in src/config/loader.ts:
   - resolveRepoPath(repoPath: string, configDir: string): string

2. Path resolution logic:
   - Expand ~ to os.homedir() at the start of paths
   - Resolve relative paths against the config file's directory (not cwd)
   - Normalize paths using path.resolve() and path.normalize()
   - Handle Windows/Unix path separators

3. Post-validation processing in loadConfig:
   - For each repo in config.repos:
     a. Resolve the path using resolveRepoPath
     b. Check if resolved path exists using fs.access or fs.stat
     c. If path doesn't exist: console.warn('Warning: Repo path does not exist: {path}')
     d. DO NOT throw/crash - continue processing other repos
   - Return Config with resolved paths in repos

4. Examples:
   - ~/code/app → /Users/john/code/app
   - ./repos/api (config at ~/config/gitradar.yml) → /Users/john/config/repos/api
   - /absolute/path → /absolute/path (unchanged)

5. Write tests covering all resolution scenarios including edge cases.
subtasks.testStrategy: Write vitest tests for: (1) tilde expansion works correctly (mock os.homedir); (2) relative paths resolve against config dir, not cwd; (3) absolute paths remain unchanged; (4) path normalization handles double slashes, trailing slashes; (5) non-existent repo paths trigger console.warn but don't crash; (6) valid paths pass through without warnings; (7) mixed valid/invalid paths process all repos. Mock fs.access/stat and console.warn to verify behavior.

tasks.expansionPrompt: Break down into: (1) File classifier with ordered regex rule matching (storybook > test > config > app priority), (2) Visual encoding constants for file types (chars/colors), (3) YAML config loader with Zod validation, (4) Path resolution for repo paths including ~ expansion and relative path handling. Each with comprehensive tests.

tasks.title: UI Foundation: Format Utilities and Basic Components
tasks.description: Build the foundational UI utilities for number formatting, ANSI handling, and basic rendering components (legend, sparkline, inline bar, banner). These are reused by all chart and table components.
tasks.details:
1. Create `src/ui/format.ts`:
   - fmt(n: number): string - Format 1234 → "1.2K", 1234567 → "1.2M"
   - delta(curr: number, prev: number): string - chalk.green("▲ 12%") or chalk.red("▼ 8%") or chalk.dim("─ 0%")
   - weekLabel(w: string): string - "2026-W08" → "Feb 17" (Monday of that week)
   - weekShort(w: string): string - "2026-W08" → "W08"
   - stripAnsi(s: string): string - Remove ANSI escape codes for width calculation
   - padRight(s: string, n: number): string - ANSI-aware right padding
   - padLeft(s: string, n: number): string - ANSI-aware left padding
   - visualWidth(s: string): number - True display width after stripping ANSI

2. Create `src/ui/legend.ts`:
   - renderLegend(items: LegendItem[], options?): string
   - LegendItem: { label, color: ChalkFn, char?: string }
   - Support inline mode for file type legends: "■ app  ■ test  ■ config  ■ storybook"
   - Support team legends with line styles: "── Platform ── Product ·· FrontendCo"

3. Create `src/ui/sparkline.ts`:
   - sparkline(values: number[], options?): string
   - Use block characters: ▁▂▃▄▅▆▇█
   - Scale to min/max of values array
   - Optional color function

4. Create `src/ui/bar.ts`:
   - stackedBar(segments: Segment[], width: number): string
   - Segment: { value, color: ChalkFn, char }
   - Scale proportionally, minimum 1 char per non-zero segment

5. Create `src/ui/banner.ts`:
   - renderBanner(options: { title, subtitle?, right?, rightSub? }): string
   - Simple header with title on left, optional right-aligned text
tasks.testStrategy:
Format tests:
- Number formatting at various scales (hundreds, thousands, millions)
- Delta calculations including edge cases (prev = 0, no change)
- Week label parsing
- ANSI stripping and padding with colored strings

Component tests:
- Sparkline with increasing, decreasing, flat values
- Stacked bar proportional rendering

subtasks.title: Implement Number and Date Format Utilities
subtasks.description: Create src/ui/format.ts with fmt(), delta(), weekLabel(), and weekShort() functions for human-readable number formatting and ISO week date handling.
subtasks.details: Implement fmt(n: number): string - Format numbers with K/M suffixes (1234 → '1.2K', 1234567 → '1.2M'). Handle edge cases: 0, negative numbers, values between thresholds. Implement delta(curr: number, prev: number): string - Return chalk.green('▲ 12%') for increases, chalk.red('▼ 8%') for decreases, chalk.dim('─ 0%') for no change. Handle prev=0 edge case (avoid division by zero, show 'new' or appropriate indicator). Implement weekLabel(w: string): string - Convert '2026-W08' to 'Feb 17' (Monday of that ISO week). Use Date arithmetic to find the Monday. Implement weekShort(w: string): string - Extract 'W08' from '2026-W08'. Include chalk as a dependency for delta() coloring.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test fmt() with values: 0, 100, 999, 1000, 1234, 999999, 1000000, 1234567, negative numbers. Test delta() with curr>prev, curr<prev, curr===prev, prev===0. Test weekLabel() with W01, W52, leap year edge cases. Test weekShort() extraction.

subtasks.title: Implement ANSI-Aware String Utilities
subtasks.description: Add stripAnsi(), visualWidth(), padRight(), and padLeft() functions to src/ui/format.ts for correct width calculations with colored terminal strings.
subtasks.details: Implement stripAnsi(s: string): string - Remove ANSI escape codes using regex /\x1b\[[0-9;]*m/g for accurate width calculation. Implement visualWidth(s: string): number - Return stripAnsi(s).length, which is the true display width after removing invisible escape codes. Implement padRight(s: string, n: number): string - ANSI-aware right padding: add spaces based on visualWidth, not s.length. Formula: s + ' '.repeat(Math.max(0, n - visualWidth(s))). Implement padLeft(s: string, n: number): string - ANSI-aware left padding: prepend spaces based on visualWidth. Formula: ' '.repeat(Math.max(0, n - visualWidth(s))) + s. These are critical for table alignment where cells contain chalk-colored content.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test stripAnsi() with chalk.green('text'), nested colors, strings without ANSI codes. Test visualWidth() returns correct length for colored vs plain strings. Test padRight/padLeft with colored strings - verify padding spaces are correct when visual width differs from string length. Test edge cases: empty strings, strings longer than target width.

subtasks.title: Create Legend Renderer Component
subtasks.description: Create src/ui/legend.ts with renderLegend() function supporting inline file-type legends and styled team legends with line styles.
subtasks.details: Define LegendItem interface: { label: string; color: ChalkFn; char?: string }. Define LegendOptions interface: { inline?: boolean; style?: 'block' | 'line' }. Implement renderLegend(items: LegendItem[], options?): string. For file-type legends (inline mode with char): '■ app  ■ test  ■ config  ■ storybook' - each item renders as color(char) + ' ' + label, joined by double space. For team legends (line style): '── Platform ── Product ·· FrontendCo' - core teams use '──' prefix (solid), consultant teams use '··' prefix (dotted). Use padRight for consistent spacing between items. Support both horizontal (inline) and vertical (newline-separated) layouts.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test inline legend with file-type items matching PRD spec. Test team legend with mixed core/consultant teams. Test that colors are applied correctly to chars and labels. Test empty items array. Test single item. Verify output matches exact PRD examples.

subtasks.title: Build Sparkline Component
subtasks.description: Create src/ui/sparkline.ts with sparkline() function using block characters ▁▂▃▄▅▆▇█ that scales to min/max of input values.
subtasks.details: Implement sparkline(values: number[], options?: { color?: ChalkFn }): string. Use block character array: ['▁', '▂', '▃', '▄', '▅', '▆', '▇', '█'] (8 levels, indices 0-7). Scaling algorithm: find min and max of values array, for each value compute index = Math.round((value - min) / (max - min) * 7). Handle edge cases: empty array returns empty string, single value returns middle block, all same values returns middle blocks. Apply optional color function to entire output string. The sparkline visualizes 12 weeks of per-team activity in the trends view, e.g., '▁▂▃▃▅▅▇▅▃▅▇█'.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test with increasing values [1,2,3,4,5] - should show ascending blocks. Test with decreasing values - descending blocks. Test flat values [5,5,5,5] - all same height. Test single value array. Test empty array. Test with color function applied. Verify min value maps to ▁ and max to █.

subtasks.title: Implement Stacked Bar and Banner Components
subtasks.description: Create src/ui/bar.ts with stackedBar() function for inline table cells, and src/ui/banner.ts with renderBanner() for view headers.
subtasks.details: For src/ui/bar.ts - Implement stackedBar(segments: Segment[], width: number): string. Segment: { value: number; color: ChalkFn; char: string }. Calculate total = sum of all segment values. For each segment: chars = Math.round(segment.value / total * width), minimum 1 char if value > 0. Concatenate color(char.repeat(chars)) for each segment. Handle edge case: all zeros returns empty or placeholder. For src/ui/banner.ts - Implement renderBanner(options: { title: string; subtitle?: string; right?: string; rightSub?: string }): string. Render title on left (bold), optional subtitle below in dim. Right-align optional right text and rightSub. Use padRight/padLeft for alignment. Calculate spacing to push right content to terminal edge. Return multi-line string with header formatting.
subtasks.testStrategy: Stacked bar tests: segments with proportional values render correct char counts, minimum 1 char per non-zero segment, empty segments handled, total width matches target. Banner tests: title only, title with subtitle, right-aligned text, full options. Verify alignment using ANSI-aware padding. Test banner with various terminal widths.

tasks.expansionPrompt: Break down into: (1) Format utilities (fmt, delta, weekLabel, weekShort) in src/ui/format.ts, (2) ANSI handling utilities (stripAnsi, padRight, padLeft, visualWidth), (3) Legend renderer with inline and styled modes, (4) Sparkline component using block characters ▁▂▃▄▅▆▇█, (5) Inline stacked bar and banner components. Each with unit tests.

tasks.title: Data Storage Layer
tasks.description: Implement the aggregated data store (commits-by-filetype.json) and scan state management (scan-state.json) with atomic writes, merge logic, and pruning capabilities.
tasks.details:
1. Create `src/store/scan-state.ts`:
   - loadScanState(): Promise<ScanState> - Load or return empty state
   - saveScanState(state: ScanState): Promise<void> - Atomic write (.tmp + rename)
   - getRepoState(state, repoName): RepoScanState | undefined
   - updateRepoState(state, repoName, update): ScanState - Immutable update
   - isStale(repoState, stalenessMinutes): boolean
   - rotateHashes(recentHashes, newHashes, maxSize=500): string[]

2. Create `src/store/commits-by-filetype.ts`:
   - loadCommitsData(): Promise<CommitsByFiletype> - Load or return empty
   - saveCommitsData(data: CommitsByFiletype): Promise<void> - Atomic write
   - mergeRecords(existing: Record[], incoming: Record[]): Record[]
     - Key: "member::week::repo"
     - Merge metrics: sum commits, activeDays, filetype counts

   - shouldAutoPrune(data): boolean - >50MB or >100K records
   - getStoreStats(data): { recordCount, orgCount, teamCount, oldestWeek, newestWeek }

3. Implement atomic file operations:

   - Use fs.rename() for atomic move
   - Handle errors gracefully (log and continue if possible)

- Save and reload state
- Staleness calculation

Commits store tests:

subtasks.title: Implement scan state loading and saving with atomic writes
subtasks.description: Create src/store/scan-state.ts with loadScanState() and saveScanState() functions. loadScanState reads scan-state.json or returns an empty state object with version 1 and empty repos record. saveScanState implements atomic writes by first writing to a .tmp file then using fs.rename() to atomically move it to the final location.
subtasks.details:
1. Create src/store/scan-state.ts importing paths from store/paths.ts and ScanState type from types/schema.ts

2. Implement loadScanState(): Promise<ScanState>
   - Use getScanStatePath() for file location
   - Try fs.readFile with utf-8 encoding
   - JSON.parse the contents
   - Return empty state on ENOENT: { version: 1, repos: {} }
   - Throw on other errors (malformed JSON, permission errors)

3. Implement saveScanState(state: ScanState): Promise<void>
   - Build .tmp path: getScanStatePath() + '.tmp'
   - Call ensureDataDir() to create directories if missing
   - fs.writeFile to .tmp with JSON.stringify(state, null, 2)
   - fs.rename from .tmp to final path (atomic on POSIX)
   - Wrap in try/catch, log errors but continue if possible

4. Write tests in src/store/scan-state.test.ts:
   - Test loadScanState returns empty state when file missing
   - Test saveScanState creates file and can be reloaded
   - Test atomic write (.tmp file used, final file appears atomically)
   - Mock fs functions using vitest vi.mock
subtasks.testStrategy: Use vi.mock('fs/promises') to control file operations. Test cases: (1) loadScanState with missing file returns { version: 1, repos: {} }; (2) saveScanState writes to .tmp then renames; (3) full round-trip: save then load returns same data; (4) loadScanState throws on malformed JSON; (5) saveScanState handles ensureDataDir being called first.

subtasks.title: Implement scan state helper functions (getRepoState, updateRepoState, isStale, rotateHashes)
subtasks.description: Add helper functions to scan-state.ts for working with scan state data immutably. getRepoState retrieves a repo's state, updateRepoState returns a new state with updated repo data, isStale checks if a repo needs rescanning, and rotateHashes maintains a rolling window of recent commit hashes.
subtasks.details:
1. Implement getRepoState(state: ScanState, repoName: string): RepoScanState | undefined
   - Return state.repos[repoName] or undefined if not present
   - Simple lookup, no mutation

2. Implement updateRepoState(state: ScanState, repoName: string, update: Partial<RepoScanState>): ScanState
   - Return a NEW ScanState object (immutable update pattern)
   - Merge update into existing repo state: { ...state.repos[repoName], ...update }

subtasks.title: Parse git log output with --numstat format extraction
subtasks.description: Create git log parsing utilities that handle the specific output format: commit header line (%H|%ae|%an|%aI) followed by numstat lines (insertions, deletions, filepath) for each changed file.
subtasks.details:
1. Define git log format string: --format="%H|%ae|%an|%aI" which produces:
   - %H = commit hash (40 chars)
   - %ae = author email
   - %an = author name
   - %aI = author date ISO 8601

2. Create parseGitLogOutput(output: string): ParsedCommit[]
   - Split output by blank lines to separate commits
   - For each commit block:
     a. First line is header: parse with regex /^([a-f0-9]{40})\|(.+?)\|(.+?)\|(.+)$/
     b. Remaining lines are numstat: parse /^(\d+|-)\t(\d+|-)\t(.+)$/
        - '-' means binary file (treat as 0 insertions/deletions)
     c. Collect into ParsedCommit: { hash, email, name, date, files: { insertions, deletions, path }[] }

3. Handle edge cases:
   - Empty output (no commits) → return []
   - Binary files show '-' for insertions/deletions → treat as 0
   - File renames show 'old => new' format
   - Merge commits excluded by --no-merges flag

4. Write tests with realistic git log output samples
subtasks.testStrategy: Test parseGitLogOutput with: (1) single commit with multiple files; (2) multiple commits separated by blank lines; (3) binary files showing '-' for stats; (4) empty output returns empty array; (5) file renames with '=>' in path; (6) commit with no file changes; (7) special characters in author name/email. subtasks.title: Implement Column Sizing Algorithm with Fixed/MinWidth/Flex Distribution
subtasks.description: Build the core column sizing algorithm for the table component that handles fixed widths, minimum widths, and proportional flex distribution of remaining space.
subtasks.details:
Create src/ui/table.ts with the column sizing algorithm:

   - flex?: number (proportional weight)
   - format?: (value: any, row: Record<string, any>) => string
   - headerColor?: (s: string) => string

2. Implement calculateColumnWidths(columns: Column[], maxWidth: number): number[]
   - Step 1: Assign fixed widths where column.width is set
   - Step 2: Calculate used space from fixed columns
   - Step 3: Apply minWidth floors to non-fixed columns
   - Step 4: Calculate remaining space after minWidths
   - Step 5: Calculate total flex weight from columns with flex property
   - Step 6: Distribute remaining space proportionally by flex weight
   - Step 7: Clamp widths to maxWidth constraints

3. Handle edge cases:
   - No flex columns: fixed widths only
   - All flex columns: pure proportional
   - Overflow: when minWidths exceed maxWidth, scale proportionally
   - Zero flex: column gets only its minWidth

4. Use visualWidth() from format.ts for any content-based width detection

subtasks.title: Build ANSI-Aware Cell Rendering with Truncation and Ellipsis
subtasks.description: Implement cell content rendering that correctly handles ANSI escape codes, uses stripAnsi() for width calculations, and applies truncation with ellipsis when content exceeds column width.
subtasks.details:
Add cell rendering functions to src/ui/table.ts:

1. Import stripAnsi, visualWidth, padRight, padLeft from ui/format.ts

2. Implement truncateWithEllipsis(content: string, maxWidth: number): string
   - Use visualWidth() to get true display width (ignoring ANSI codes)
   - If content fits, return unchanged
   - If content is too long:
     a. Find truncation point using character-by-character measurement
     b. Account for ANSI escape sequences (they contribute 0 visual width)
     c. Append '…' (single-char ellipsis, not '...')
     d. Ensure final visualWidth <= maxWidth

3. Implement renderCell(value: any, column: Column, row: Record<string, any>, width: number): string
   - Apply column.format function if provided (may return ANSI-colored string)
   - Convert value to string if not already
   - Apply truncateWithEllipsis to fit within width
   - Apply alignment padding using ANSI-aware padRight/padLeft/padCenter

4. Implement alignContent(content: string, width: number, align: 'left'|'right'|'center'): string
   - left: padRight(content, width)
   - right: padLeft(content, width)
   - center: calculate left padding as floor((width - visualWidth) / 2), then padRight

5. Critical: All width calculations must use visualWidth() to ignore ANSI escape codes, since cells may contain chalk-colored content, sparklines, or inline bars
subtasks.testStrategy: Write vitest tests: (1) Plain text truncation at exact boundary; (2) ANSI-colored text truncation preserves colors correctly up to truncation point; (3) Ellipsis added when content exceeds width; (4) Content exactly at width is NOT truncated; (5) Left/right/center alignment with plain text; (6) Left/right/center alignment with ANSI-colored text; (7) Format function output is correctly processed; (8) Nested ANSI codes (bold + color) handled; (9) Unicode characters (emoji, CJK) width handling if applicable.

subtasks.description: Create the line chart component's core rendering engine with Y-axis scale calculation from min/max values across all series, and row-by-row rendering from top (high values) to bottom (low values).
subtasks.details:

2. Implement calculateYScale(series: LineSeries[]): { min: number; max: number; range: number }
   - Flatten all series values to find global min and max
   - Calculate range = max - min
   - Handle edge case: all same values (range = 0, use single middle row)

3. Implement valueToRow(value: number, scale: { min, max }, height: number): number
   - Map value to row index where row 0 is TOP (highest values)
   - Formula: height - 1 - Math.round((value - min) / range * (height - 1))
   - Clamp to valid row range [0, height-1]

4. Implement buildChartGrid(series: LineSeries[], width: number, height: number, scale): string[][]
   - Create 2D array: grid[row][col] initialized with spaces

subtasks.title: Build view stack navigator with push/pop/replace/quit actions
subtasks.description: Implement the NavigationAction type and runNavigator loop that manages a view stack, executing views and processing navigation actions.
subtasks.details:
Create `src/views/navigator.ts` with:

1. **NavigationAction type:**
   ```typescript
   type NavigationAction =
     | { type: "push"; view: ViewFn }
     | { type: "pop" }
     | { type: "replace"; view: ViewFn }
     | { type: "quit" };
   ```

2. **ViewFn type:**
   ```typescript
   type ViewFn = (ctx: ViewContext) => Promise<NavigationAction>;
   ```

3. **runNavigator(initialView: ViewFn, ctx: ViewContext): Promise<void>**
   - Initialize stack with `[initialView]`
   - Main loop while stack.length > 0:
     - Get current view: `stack[stack.length - 1]`
     - Call `console.clear()` before each view
     - Execute view: `const action = await currentView(ctx)`
     - Process action:
       - `push`: `stack.push(action.view)`
       - `pop`: `stack.pop()`
       - `replace`: `stack[stack.length - 1] = action.view`
       - `quit`: `return` (exit loop)
   - Handle empty stack gracefully (just return)

4. **Action factory helpers:**
   - `push(view: ViewFn): NavigationAction`
   - `pop(): NavigationAction`
   - `replace(view: ViewFn): NavigationAction`
   - `quit(): NavigationAction`

5. **Error handling:**
   - Wrap view execution in try/catch
   - On error, print error message and return quit action
   - Preserve error context for debugging
subtasks.testStrategy: Navigator tests: Create mock views that return specific actions, verify push adds to stack, pop removes from stack, replace substitutes current, quit exits loop. Test empty stack handling, error recovery, and view execution order.

   - `--prune <days>` - Remove records older than N days
   - `--store-stats` - Print data file stats and exit
   - `--reset` - Delete data files and start fresh
   - `--staleness <min>` - Skip repo if scanned recently (default: config or 60)

subtasks.title: Implement startup flow orchestration
subtasks.description: Build the main startup sequence: load config, load existing data, check staleness, scan repos with progress display, merge data, save atomically, then launch the appropriate view.
subtasks.details:
Add to `src/cli.ts` the main startup orchestration:

1. **Main flow function:**
   ```typescript
   async function main(options: ParsedOptions): Promise<void>
   ```

2. **Startup sequence:**
   a. Parse CLI args (already done in step 3)
   b. Load config (or use demo mode if --demo)
   c. Load scan-state.json + commits-by-filetype.json
   d. Print store stats: "Store: N records · X orgs · Y teams · last scan: Zm ago"
   e. If not --force-scan, check staleness per repo
   f. Scan repos needing update with ora progress spinner:
      - "· frontend-app: fresh (12m ago)" for skipped
      - "✓ frontend-app: +12 commits → 6 new records" for scanned
   g. Merge new records into existing data
   h. Atomic save both JSON files (write .tmp then rename)
   i. Launch appropriate view based on command

3. **Progress display with ora:**
   - Show spinner during git log execution
   - Update text with current repo name
   - Success/fail symbols for completion

4. **Error handling:**
   - Config not found: helpful message with init suggestion
   - Invalid config: show Zod validation errors
   - Git errors: warn but continue with other repos
   - File permission errors: fail with clear message

5. **Command routing:**
   - default: launch dashboard via navigator
   - scan: scan and exit
   - trends: launch trends view directly
   - init: run interactive config creator

tasks.title: Define ReposRegistry Zod Schema
tasks.description: Create Zod schemas for the workspace-based repos.yml registry file in src/types/schema.ts. This schema defines workspaces containing repos, plus shared groups and tags definitions.
tasks.details:
1. Add new schemas to `src/types/schema.ts`:

```ts
const WorkspaceRepoSchema = z.object({
  name: z.string(),
  path: z.string(),
  group: z.string().optional().default('default'),
  tags: z.array(z.string()).optional().default([]),
});

const WorkspaceSchema = z.object({
  label: z.string().optional(),
  repos: z.array(WorkspaceRepoSchema),
});

const ReposRegistrySchema = z.object({
  workspaces: z.record(z.string(), WorkspaceSchema),
  groups: z.record(z.string(), z.object({ label: z.string().optional() })).optional().default({}),
  tags: z.record(z.string(), z.object({ label: z.string().optional() })).optional().default({}),
});
```

2. Export inferred TypeScript types:
   - `WorkspaceRepo = z.infer<typeof WorkspaceRepoSchema>`
   - `Workspace = z.infer<typeof WorkspaceSchema>`
   - `ReposRegistry = z.infer<typeof ReposRegistrySchema>`

3. Note: WorkspaceRepo differs from RepoSchema - it has `tags` array instead of single `group` (workspace repos can have multiple tags).

4. The schema supports the file format:
```yaml
workspaces:
  engineering:
    label: Engineering
    repos:
      - name: frontend-app
        path: ~/code/frontend-app
        group: web
        tags: [feature]
groups:
  web: { label: Web }
tags:
  feature: { label: Feature }
```
tasks.testStrategy:
Write unit tests in src/__tests__/schema.test.ts:
- Valid ReposRegistry with multiple workspaces parses correctly
- Single workspace with minimal config (just repos) validates
- Empty workspaces object fails validation
- Repos without optional fields get defaults (group='default', tags=[])
- Groups and tags records are optional and default to empty objects

tasks.title: Build Workspace Selector Component
tasks.description: Create src/config/workspace-selector.ts with interactive workspace selection using @inquirer/prompts when multiple workspaces are available.
tasks.details:
1. Create `src/config/workspace-selector.ts`:

```ts
import { select } from '@inquirer/prompts';

interface WorkspaceChoice {
  name: string;
  label?: string;
  repoCount: number;
  sourceType: 'global' | 'project';
  sourcePath: string;
}

export async function selectWorkspace(
  workspaces: WorkspaceChoice[],
  preselected?: string
): Promise<WorkspaceChoice | null>
```

2. Selection logic:
   - If preselected workspace name matches exactly one workspace, return it
   - If only 1 workspace total, return it automatically (no prompt)
   - If 0 workspaces, return null
   - If multiple workspaces, show interactive select prompt

3. Prompt format groups by source:
```
Select workspace:

  Global (~/.agentx/repos.yml)
    1. engineering        (12 repos)
    2. data-dept          (4 repos)

  Project (/Users/you/code/acme/.agentx/repos.yml)
    3. client-acme        (3 repos)
    4. engineering        (6 repos)

> _
```

4. Use @inquirer/prompts select() with choices structured as:
   - Group by sourceType, show source path in group header
   - Each choice shows: name, repo count in parentheses
   - Return the full WorkspaceChoice object

5. Handle Ctrl+C gracefully (return null or throw to exit)

tasks.description: Create function to merge workspace repos, groups, and tags with orgs from config.yml, producing a unified Config object for the application.
tasks.details:

```ts
export function buildConfigFromWorkspace(
  workspace: LoadedWorkspace,
  registries: RegistrySource[],
  configYml?: Config  // Optional existing config.yml for orgs
): Config
```

2. Merge logic:
   - Repos: Use workspace.repos (converted to RepoSchema format)
   - Groups: Merge from workspace's source registry
   - Tags: Merge from workspace's source registry
   - Orgs: Use from configYml if provided, otherwise empty array
   - Settings: Use from configYml if provided, otherwise defaults

3. WorkspaceRepo → RepoSchema conversion:
```ts
function convertWorkspaceRepo(wr: WorkspaceRepo): Repo {
  return {
    path: wr.path,
    name: wr.name,
    group: wr.group,  // Note: WorkspaceRepo.tags are ignored here
  };
}
```

4. Update config loader to:
   - Accept optional flag to skip repos when workspace is active
   - Or create new function loadOrgsFromConfig() that only loads orgs/settings

5. When repos.yml workspace is active and config.yml has repos:
   - Log warning: 'Note: repos.yml workspace takes precedence; config.yml repos ignored'
   - Only use repos from workspace

6. Final Config object structure matches existing ConfigSchema for compatibility with all downstream code

tasks.description: Extend ConfigSchema with optional workspace field that allows pre-selecting a workspace in config.yml, skipping the interactive prompt.
tasks.details:
1. Update ConfigSchema in `src/types/schema.ts`:

```ts
export const ConfigSchema = z.object({
  workspace: z.string().optional(),  // NEW: pre-select workspace
  repos: z.array(RepoSchema),
  orgs: z.array(OrgSchema),
  groups: z.record(z.string(), z.object({ label: z.string().optional() })).optional().default({}),
  tags: z.record(z.string(), z.object({ label: z.string().optional() })).optional().default({}),
  settings: z.object({
    weeks_back: z.number().optional().default(12),
    staleness_minutes: z.number().optional().default(60),
  }).optional().default({ weeks_back: 12, staleness_minutes: 60 }),
});
```

2. Update Config type inference to include workspace field.

3. Integrate into workspace selection (in cli.ts):
```ts
// Use workspace from: CLI flag > config.yml > prompt
const workspaceName = opts.workspace ?? config?.workspace;
```

4. This allows users to set default workspace in config.yml:
```yaml
# ~/.agentx/gitradar/config.yml
workspace: engineering

orgs:
  - name: Acme Corp
    type: core
    teams: [...]
```

5. Load config.yml early (before workspace selection) to check for workspace field, then reload/merge after workspace is selected.

tasks.title: Implement Backward Compatibility and Edge Cases
tasks.description: Handle all edge cases for repos.yml adoption including missing files, invalid configs, and migration from config.yml-only setups.
tasks.details:
1. Edge case handling in CLI startup:

   a. No repos.yml exists (global or project), no config.yml:
      - Show error: 'No configuration found. Run gitradar init or create config.yml'
   
   b. No repos.yml exists, config.yml exists with repos:
      - Use config.yml repos (existing behavior, full backward compat)
      - No workspace prompt shown
   
   c. repos.yml exists with workspaces, config.yml has repos:
      - Use repos.yml workspace, ignore config.yml repos
      - Log: 'Using workspace from repos.yml (config.yml repos ignored)'
   
   d. repos.yml exists but has 0 workspaces:
      - Fall back to config.yml if it has repos
      - Otherwise error
   
   e. --demo flag:
      - Skip workspace selection entirely
      - Generate demo data as before

2. Graceful warnings:
   - Invalid repos.yml YAML syntax: 'Warning: Could not parse ~/.agentx/repos.yml: {error}'
   - Invalid repos.yml schema: 'Warning: Invalid repos.yml format: {validation errors}'
   - Continue with other sources or config.yml fallback

3. Document migration path:
   - Existing users with config.yml work unchanged
   - Create repos.yml to adopt workspaces
   - Move repos/groups/tags from config.yml to repos.yml gradually

4. Update `gitradar init` command output to mention repos.yml option
tasks.testStrategy:

- No repos.yml + valid config.yml uses config.yml repos
- Valid repos.yml + config.yml with repos uses workspace, logs warning
- Empty workspaces in repos.yml falls back to config.yml
- Invalid repos.yml YAML logs warning, continues
- Invalid repos.yml schema logs warning, continues
- --demo flag bypasses all workspace logic
- Verify init command output mentions repos.yml
